Uses of Class
org.moeaframework.util.weights.NormalBoundaryDivisions

Packages that use NormalBoundaryDivisions
Package
Description
org.moeaframework.algorithm
Implementations of various algorithms and support tools for working with algorithms.
org.moeaframework.util.weights
Routines for generating weights for decomposition and reference point methods.

    Methods in org.moeaframework.algorithm that return NormalBoundaryDivisions
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    NormalBoundaryDivisions
    DBEA.getDivisions()
    Returns the number of divisions used to generate reference points.
    NormalBoundaryDivisions
    ReferencePointNondominatedSortingPopulation.getDivisions()
    Returns the number of divisions used to create the reference points.
    NormalBoundaryDivisions
    ReferenceVectorGuidedPopulation.getDivisions()
    Returns the number of divisions used to generate the reference vectors.
    Methods in org.moeaframework.algorithm with parameters of type NormalBoundaryDivisions
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    protected static final int
    NSGAIII.getInitialPopulationSize(Problem problem, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Returns the population size, which is the number of reference points rounded up to the nearest multiple of 4.
    void
    DBEA.setDivisions(NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Sets the number of divisions used to generate reference points.
    Constructors in org.moeaframework.algorithm with parameters of type NormalBoundaryDivisions
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    DBEA(Problem problem, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Constructs a new instance of the DBEA algorithm with the given number of divisions.
     
    NSGAIII(Problem problem, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Creates a new NSGA-III instance with the given number of reference point divisions.
     
    ReferencePointNondominatedSortingPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Constructs an empty population that maintains the rank attribute for its solutions.
     
    ReferencePointNondominatedSortingPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, Iterable<? extends Solution> iterable)
    Constructs a new population with the specified solutions that maintains the rank attribute for its solutions.
     
    ReferencePointNondominatedSortingPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, DominanceComparator comparator)
    Constructs an empty population that maintains the rank attribute for its solutions.
     
    ReferencePointNondominatedSortingPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, DominanceComparator comparator, Iterable<? extends Solution> iterable)
    Constructs a new population with the specified solutions that maintains the rank attribute for its solutions.
     
    ReferenceVectorGuidedPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Constructs a new populationf or RVEA using default settings.
     
    ReferenceVectorGuidedPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, double alpha)
    Constructs a new population for RVEA.
     
    ReferenceVectorGuidedPopulation(int numberOfObjectives, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, double alpha, Iterable<? extends Solution> iterable)
    Constructs a new population for RVEA.
     
    RVEA(Problem problem, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions, int maxGeneration)
    Constructs a new instance of RVEA with default settings.
     
    UNSGAIII(Problem problem, NormalBoundaryDivisions divisions)
    Creates a new U-NSGA-III instance with the given number of reference point divisions.

    Returns the default number of divisions based on the number of objectives.

    NormalBoundaryDivisions.tryFromProperties(TypedProperties properties)
    Reads the division properties, if set.
